flashcards.front: What is the purpose of abstract classes in Python?
flashcards.back: Abstract classes provide a way to define a class that cannot be instantiated on its own and is meant to be inherited by other classes.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between abstract classes and interfaces in Python?
flashcards.back: Abstract classes can have both abstract and concrete methods, while interfaces can only have abstract methods.

flashcards.front: How do you define an abstract class in Python?
flashcards.back: You define an abstract class in Python by using the 'ABC' class from the 'abc' module and decorating the abstract methods with the '@abstractmethod' decorator.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of interfaces in Python?
flashcards.back: Interfaces provide a way to define a contract that must be implemented by any class that implements it.

flashcards.front: Can you instantiate an abstract class in Python?
flashcards.back: No, you cannot instantiate an abstract class in Python.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a concrete class and an abstract class in Python?
flashcards.back: A concrete class can be instantiated on its own, while an abstract class cannot be instantiated on its own and is meant to be inherited by other classes.

flashcards.front: How do you implement an interface in Python?
flashcards.back: You implement an interface in Python by creating a class that inherits from the interface and implements all of its abstract methods.

flashcards.front: Can an abstract class have concrete methods in Python?
flashcards.back: Yes, an abstract class can have concrete methods in Python.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the '@abstractmethod' decorator in Python?
flashcards.back: The '@abstractmethod' decorator is used to define an abstract method in an abstract class.

flashcards.front: Can an interface have concrete methods in Python?
flashcards.back: No, an interface cannot have concrete methods in Python.

flashcards.front: How do you define an interface in Python?
flashcards.back: You define an interface in Python by using the 'ABC' class from the 'abc' module and decorating all methods with the '@abstractmethod' decorator.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a class and an interface in Python?
flashcards.back: A class can have both abstract and concrete methods, while an interface can only have abstract methods.

flashcards.front: Can a class implement multiple interfaces in Python?
flashcards.back: Yes, a class can implement multiple interfaces in Python.

flashcards.front: Can an abstract class inherit from another abstract class in Python?
flashcards.back: Yes, an abstract class can inherit from another abstract class in Python.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the 'ABC' class in Python?
flashcards.back: The 'ABC' class is used to define an abstract base class in Python.
